This classification places Gymnosphaera melanorhachis among the vascular, non-flowering plants. The order Polypodiales and the family Lindsaeaceae further categorize this species. As a member of the kingdom Plantae, Gymnosphaera melanorhachis is a type of plant that is widely distributed across the globe.
uses: Gymnosphaera melanorhachis, commonly known as the black- spined club moss, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and stems are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat wounds and skin conditions. In some parts of the world, the plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
